title: fraxinus rufescens
intro: Fraxinus rufescens, also known as the lance-leaf ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus rufescens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Fraxinus rufescens can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, salves, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
